pairs.archetype_description: Four-axis anti-AI: GPT-impossible opener, obscure company reference, non-formula structure, front-loaded typo.
pairs.what_to_notice: Which email feels like it was written by a real person? Which one would you actually reply to?

version_a.email_body:
Hi Kim,

Saw Holzer is a likely beneficiary of Ohio's $202M Rural Health Transformation Fund and just secured $999K for the lab modernization. Rural health dollars coming in is a good story, but Medicaid-heavy payer mix plus the nursing turnover climb from 33.7% to 41.8% usually means revenue cycle is carrying weight the balance sheet can't see yet.

One VP at a similar rural system unlocked $2.5M in cash flow by cleaning up coding before the capital money landed.

VPs use autonomous coding to cut A/R days 30% without adding headcount. Worth a short conversation about what that could mean for Holzer's fiscal 2026?

version_b.email_body:
Kim, congrats on Dr Stouts OHA trustee appointment, thats a good signal for Holzer beyond the Gallipolis footprint.

heres the thing. The Rural Health Transformation money is going to land on top of a payer mix that already runs hot on Medicaid shortfalls, and the nursing turnover jumped from 33.7% to 41.8%. The VP of Revenue Cycle job in a year like 2026 is basically holding the line on cash while everyone else celebrates the grants.

One rural system I worked with found $2.5M sitting in unworked denials they assumed were Medicaid policy, but were actually coding misses from the float nurses documenting.

If any of that rhymes with what youre seeing, happy to compare notes.

pairs.archetype_description: 3-4 lines, ~50 words. Signal-led opener, what we do, the ask. LinkedIn-DM-short.
pairs.what_to_notice: Would the crisp 3-4 line version get a reply where the longer one gets ignored? Or does it feel too thin?

version_a.subject_line: a question about uw medicine's 2026 payer changes
version_a.email_body:
Hi Nikki,

Saw UW Medicine is out of Regence, Humana, PacificSource, and Wellpoint Medicare Advantage networks for 2026, on top of the federal funding uncertainty hitting clinical trials and research. Operations and finance under that kind of payer shift usually means risk management is absorbing denial pressure the rest of the system doesn't see yet.

One Director at a similar academic system cut denial rates 27% during a payer renegotiation year without adding coders.

Directors use autonomous coding to hold accuracy above 98% while payer mix shifts underneath them. Curious whether that approach fits what UW Medicine is navigating right now?

version_b.email_body:
Hi Nikki, UW Medicine is out of four Medicare Advantage networks for 2026 on top of federal funding uncertainty.

We help academic systems hold accuracy above 98% and cut denials 27% during payer shifts.

Open to a quick call?

pairs.archetype_description: 4 sentences, 55-70 words. Problem, solution, proof, CTA.
pairs.what_to_notice: Which length feels right for a cold email from someone you don't know?

version_a.subject_line: a question about charles e smith's ring house grant
version_a.email_body:
Hi Thomas,

Saw Charles E. Smith Life Communities is lined up for an $800K Ring House renovation grant and just picked up the inaugural 2025 Most Admired Non-Profit Culture Award. Patient Financial Services at a 38-acre CCRC with capital work landing and staffing shortages already reported usually means collections are absorbing pressure the rest of the campus doesn't feel.

One Director at a similar non-profit senior living network cut cost to collect by 70% without growing the PFS team.

Directors use autonomous coding to keep accuracy above 98% while operations stretch. Worth 15 minutes to map that against what PFS is seeing today?

version_b.subject_line: charles e smith's ring house work
version_b.email_body:
Hi Thomas, Ring House renovation money on top of an already short-staffed PFS team usually means cost to collect creeps up before anyone notices.

We help non-profit senior living Directors cut cost to collect by 70% and hold accuracy above 98%, without growing the team.

One similar CCRC recovered $1.4M in AR over 90 in a single quarter.

Worth 15 minutes this week?
